chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics SRV inline viscometer with a Varinline flange, detailing its features, applications, and certifications.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

SRV Varinline: Inline viscometer with Varinline flange

Modified on: Fri, 25 Oct, 2024 at 7:10 PM

What products are involved in this article?
SRV Inline viscometer (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and Varinline installation process.

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the SRV inline viscometer with Varinline connection, its uses, and installation options._(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410684)

1. Overview

The SRV is a Rheonics sensor for inline viscosity measurement. Read more on SRV here (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/).

Figure 1 shows a flush variant SRV inline viscometer with a Varinline Process Connection. This design configuration can used to make the SRV sensor probe compatible with different kinds of GEA Varinline housings (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp), which are mostly used for the installation of hygienic instruments in hygienic or sanitary applications.

The SRV probe with a Varinline connection can be ordered with an EHEDG or 3-A certification. Learn more
key_facts: The SRV Varinline is an inline viscometer with a Varinline flange.
The SRV is a Rheonics sensor used for inline viscosity measurement.
The article provides an overview of the SRV inline viscometer with Varinline connection, its uses, and installation options.
A flush variant SRV inline viscometer with a Varinline Process Connection is shown in Figure 1.
The SRV sensor probe's design makes it compatible with various GEA Varinline housings.
GEA Varinline housings are primarily used for installing hygienic instruments in hygienic or sanitary applications.
The SRV probe with a Varinline connection is available with EHEDG or 3-A certification.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the advantages and provides dimensional specifications for the Rheonics SRV-X4-VNM Flush Probe with a Varinline N Machined Plug, highlighting its hygienic design and installation details.
product_names: Flush Probe with Varinline N Machined Plug
SRV Varinline flush probe with Varinline N Machined Plug
chunks.extracted_text:
about these certifications here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412134-ehedg-and-3-a).

The advantages of using the SRV sensor with a Varinline process connection in GEA housings are that it:

*   Removes dead zones around the sensing element
*   Ensures easy cleaning and sterilization through standard CIP (Clean in place) procedure.
*   Allows perpendicular installation in small lines such as DN40 or DN50*.

*Installation in DN40 or DN50 GEA Housings requires the use of a machined plug from Rheonics. This machined plug has a cut that maintains the SRV sensing area unobstructed. This machined plug is hygienic but not certified with EHEDG or 3-A. See more in section 2 of this article.

Units: mm [inch]

SRV-X4-VNM (Flush Probe with Varinline N Machined Plug)

(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VNM.pdf)

Figure 1. SRV Varinline flush probe with Varinline N Machined Plug dimensions.
key_facts: The SRV sensor with a Varinline process connection in GEA housings removes dead zones around the sensing element.
The SRV sensor ensures easy cleaning and sterilization through standard CIP (Clean in place) procedure.
The SRV sensor allows perpendicular installation in small lines such as DN40 or DN50.
Installation in DN40 or DN50 GEA Housings requires a machined plug from Rheonics.
The Rheonics machined plug maintains the SRV sensing area unobstructed and is hygienic.
warnings_notes: Installation in DN40 or DN50 GEA Housings requires the use of a machined plug from Rheonics. This machined plug has a cut that maintains the SRV sensing area unobstructed. This machined plug is hygienic but not certified with EHEDG or 3-A. See more in section 2 of this article.

A Varinline connection is composed of a Plug, an O-ring seal, and a Clamp. In case the SRV Varinline sensor is used, this replaces the plug.

The SRV Varinline sensor probe can be installed in different GEA housings or adapters like the ones listed in the next table.

Table 2: Suitable GEA housings or adapters for the SRV Varinline sensor.

| GEA Variant                                                                                                                                                                                               | Description | Referral Image |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|----------------|
| (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/) |             |                |
key_facts: CAD files are available for Rheonics SRV-X4-Varinline-N, SRV-X4-VNM, and SRV-X2-VN sensors.
A standard Varinline connection comprises a Plug, an O-ring seal, and a Clamp.
When an SRV Varinline sensor is used, it replaces the plug in the Varinline connection.
The SRV Varinline sensor probe is compatible with various GEA housings and adapters.
GEA offers varinline-pocket-free instruments that can serve as suitable housings or adapters for the SRV Varinline sensor.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes two types of GEA Varinline housings: standard Varinline Housings and Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA), detailing their features and installation requirements.
chunks.extracted_text:
Varinline Housings
Straight housing with constant cross-section.
Allows the SRV Varinline to be installed perpendicular in-line in small pipes (DN40 minimum).
GEA Varinline housing pipes can follow different norms such as ISO 11850, DIN11866, ISO1127 AME-BPE, and IPS.
GEA Reference
https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/
https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/

Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA)
Straight housing with constant cross-section and sight glass.
Allows the SRV Varinline to be installed in a Varinline housing (DN50 minimum) with a glass at the other side to see the fluid and check the probe without dismantling.
GEA Reference
https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-sight-glass-txia/
https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-sight-glass-txia/
key_facts: Varinline Housings feature a straight housing with a constant cross-section.
SRV Varinline can be installed perpendicularly in-line in small pipes (minimum DN40) using Varinline Housings.
GEA Varinline housing pipes comply with norms including ISO 11850, DIN11866, ISO1127 AME-BPE, and IPS.
The Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA) has a straight housing with a constant cross-section and an integrated sight glass.
The Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA) allows the SRV Varinline to be installed in a Varinline housing (minimum DN50) with a glass for fluid observation and probe inspection without dismantling.

chunks.page_summary: This page details different Varinline connection flanges and their installation methods, including an example for mounting an SRV sensor probe in a DIN11850 DN40 inline flow cell.
product_names: Varinline Housing Connection Flange (Type U and U-S)
DIN11850 DN40 - Inline Flow Cell DN40
chunks.extracted_text:
Varinline Tank connection flange (Type P)
Adapter for tanks.
Allows a flush installation of the SRV Varinline in tanks.
GEA Reference
(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)
GEA Reference
(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)

Varinline Housing Connection Flange (Type U and U-S)
Allows installations of the SRV Varinline in Tee or elbows
GEA Reference
(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)

2. Installation examples
2.1 GEA Housings (Type T)

2.1.1 DIN11850 DN40 - Inline Flow Cell DN40

This solution allows a perpendicular installation of the SRV sensor probe in a straight DN40 or 1.5” line. This installation requires a Varinline N machined plug provided by Rheonics (PLG-VNM) as the probe connection and the blind plug, as shown in Figure 2.
key_facts: The Varinline Tank connection flange (Type P) serves as an adapter for tanks, enabling a flush installation of the SRV Varinline.
The Varinline Housing Connection Flange (Type U and U-S) facilitates installations of the SRV Varinline in Tee or elbow configurations.
A perpendicular installation of the SRV sensor probe is supported in a straight DN40 or 1.5” line.
This installation requires a Varinline N machined plug, provided by Rheonics (PLG-VNM), to function as the probe connection and a blind plug.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the components required for a Rheonics SRV-X4 viscometer installation in a Varinline Housing DN40, providing suppliers, order codes, and drawing references.
product_names: Inline viscometer - SRV with Varinline N Machined Process connection
chunks.extracted_text:
The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with the Rheonics SRV. If smaller housings are required contact Rheonics Support Team.

The installation in Figure 2, requires the following components. The whole assembly can be delivered by Rheonics but the client may already have the GEA accessories or can order them separately.

*   SRV-X4 Varinline N Machined from Rheonics.
*   Varinline Housing DN40
*   Varinline Machined N Plug (PLG-VNM) from Rheonics
*   Varinline DN40 Clamps.
*   O-ring Seal.

Table 3: SRV-X4 in Varinline Housing DN40

SRV - Varinline Hygienic Installation Example

|--------------------------------------------------------|----------|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Inline viscometer - SRV with Varinline N Machined Process connection | Rheonics | SRV-X4-VNM | SRV-X4-VNM_Housing-DN40 (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VNM_Housing-DN40.pdf)                                                                        |
| Varinline Housing DN654px40*                           | GEA      | TDN40      | (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/SRV-X4-BVF_IFC-V25_DN25.pdf) |
| Machined N Plug                                        | Rheonics | PLG-VNM    |                                                                                                                                                                                     |

*Can also be the GEA Housings:

*   ISO 1127 - 33.7
*   ASME - BPE - OD 1.5”
key_facts: The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with the Rheonics SRV.

The installation shown in Figure 2 requires five specific components: SRV-X4 Varinline N Machined, Varinline Housing DN40, Varinline Machined N Plug (PLG-VNM), Varinline DN40 Clamps, and an O-ring Seal.
The complete assembly for the installation can be supplied by Rheonics, or the client can acquire GEA accessories independently.
Alternative GEA Housings can comply with ISO 1127 (33.7) or ASME - BPE - OD 1.5” standards.
The inline viscometer SRV-X4-VNM is supplied by Rheonics and uses a Varinline N Machined Process connection.
The Varinline Housing DN654px40* is supplied by GEA with order code TDN40.
warnings_notes: If smaller housings than Varinline DN40 are required, contact Rheonics Support Team.
The Varinline Housing DN654px40 can also be replaced by GEA Housings with specifications ISO 1127 - 33.7 or ASME - BPE - OD 1.5".

chunks.page_summary: This page details the flush installation of Rheonics SRV viscometers in Varinline housings, providing a dimensional drawing, installation instructions for different DN sizes, and a table with product and housing order information, including compatible standards.

Figure 4. Flush installation drawing of SRV in DN40 Varinline Housing
2.1.2 DIN11850 DN50 or DN65
The SRV can be installed in the Varinline DN50 or DN65 Housing. For the DN50 or DN65 line, the probe still requires the Machined N Plug as a process connection, but the blind plug on the housing is not required to be machined, as shown in Figure 5.

Table 4: SRV-X4 in Varinline Housing DN50

key_facts: The SRV viscometer can be installed in Varinline DN50 or DN65 Housing.
For DN50 or DN65 lines, the probe requires the Machined N Plug as a process connection.
The blind plug on the housing is not required to be machined for DN50 or DN65 installations, as shown in Figure 5.
Rheonics supplies the SRV-X4-VNM inline viscometer with Varinline N Machined Process connection.
GEA supplies the Varinline Housing DN50 with order code TDN50.
warnings_notes: Varinline Housing DN50 is compatible with ISO 1127 - 42.4, ASME - BPE - OD 2", and IPS - 2" standards.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides installation details and compatibility information for Rheonics SRV viscometers in Varinline housings, including a dimensional drawing for DN50 flush installation and guidelines for DN80 and larger housings.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics
SRV-X4-VNM Varinline N Machined
Varinline Housing DN 50
Varinline N Blind Plug
Units: mm [inch]

(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50.pdf)
Figure 5. Flush installation drawing of SRV in DN50 Varinline Housing

2.1.3 DIN11850 DN80 or bigger

If a housing DIN11850 DN80 is used, the probe doesn’t require the Machined N Plug anymore (nor the housing’s blind plug ), it can be used with the common Varinline N plug as a process connection, see an example in Figure 6.

When a bigger housing is used (e.g., DN100) the SRV short probe can be used. The SRV short (SRV-X2 (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410684-srv-x2-custom-flange )) would have an advantage over the flush variant (SRV-X4 (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411682-srv-x4-flush-sensor-for-hygienic-and-sanitary-applications )) because the sensing element will be closer to the middle of the pipe.

Table 5: SRV-X4 in Varinline housing DN80
SRV - Varinline Hygienic Installation Example
Description | Supplier | Order code | Drawing
Inline viscometer - SRV | Rheonics | SRV-X4-VN | SRV-X4-VN Housing-DN80 (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VN_Housing-DN80.pdf)
Varinline Housing DN80* | GEA (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) | TDN80 | (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/SRV-X2-BVN_DN80.pdf)

*Compatible with:
* ISO 1127 - 60.3
* ASME - BPE - OD 3”
* IPS - 3”
key_facts: Figure 5 illustrates the flush installation of an SRV viscometer in a DN50 Varinline Housing.
For a DIN11850 DN80 housing, the probe does not require the Machined N Plug and can use a common Varinline N plug as a process connection.
When a larger housing (e.g., DN100) is used, the SRV short probe (SRV-X2) offers an advantage over the flush SRV-X4 variant by positioning the sensing element closer to the middle of the pipe.
Rheonics supplies the 'Inline viscometer - SRV' with the order code 'SRV-X4-VN' for Varinline hygienic installation.
GEA supplies 'Varinline Housing DN80*' with the order code 'TDN80'.
The system is compatible with ISO 1127 - 60.3, ASME - BPE - OD 3”, and IPS - 3” standards.
warnings_notes: The 'Varinline Housing DN80*' is compatible with ISO 1127 - 60.3, ASME - BPE - OD 3”, and IPS - 3” standards.

2.1.4 Varinline Sight glass (TXIA)
The sight glass allows you to see the inside of the pipe without disassembling the sensor, it is useful when manual monitoring is desired to see if the SRV probe is clean or if it has deposits/fouling.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the installation of the SRV-X4-VNM sensor using Varinline Housing with a sight glass, including configurations with concentric reducers and a GEA tank connection flange.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics.
SRV-X4-VNM.
O-ring.
1 Varinlinle Housing DN50.
2 Varinline N Sight Glass.
Units: mm [inch].
(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50_SightGlass.pdf)
Figure 7. Flush installation drawing of SRV in DN50 Varinline Housing with sight glass.
2.1.5 Varinline Sight glass - concentric reducers.
Following the previous point, we can add concentric reducers to the housing ports (e.g. DN50-DN38 reducers), as in Figure 8, to mount the sensor in smaller lines. You can weld the concentric reducers to the line or use Tri-clamp ferrules for easy disassembly.
SRV-X4-VNM.
Varinline Housing DN50.
Concetric Reducers DN50-DN38 or less.
Sight Glass DN50.
(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/SRV-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50_SightGlass_Reducers.pdf)
Figure 8. Flush installation drawing of SRV in DN50 Varinline Housing with sight glass and concentric reducers.
2.2 GEA Tank connection flange (Type P).
The SRV can be installed in the Varinline tank connection flange which is welded into vessels with a wall thickness of up to 20 mm to avoid dead zones in a hygienic installation.
Table 7: SRV-X4 tank connection process (TPN).
key_facts: Concentric reducers can be added to housing ports (e.g., DN50-DN38) to mount the SRV sensor in smaller lines.
Concentric reducers can be welded to the line or attached using Tri-clamp ferrules for easy disassembly.
The SRV can be installed in a Varinline tank connection flange which is welded into vessels.
The Varinline tank connection flange installation supports vessel wall thicknesses up to 20 mm.
The GEA Tank connection flange (Type P) installation is used to avoid dead zones in a hygienic installation.

GEA supplies the Varinline tank connection flange with the order code TPN.
A drawing for 'SRV-X4-B Varinline N in tank' is available from Rheonics' website.
The page illustrates the Varinline tank connection process and provides an exploded view of its assembly components.
The components for the Varinline tank connection flange assembly include an SRV-X4-Varinline N sensor, a welded tank Varinline flange, an O-ring N, two grooved flanges, and four bolts.
An alternative installation option for the tank connection flange utilizes an SRV Long Insertion probe, specifically the SRV-X5 model, with further details available on the Rheonics support page.

Figure 11. Exploded view of assembly example with Varinline tank connection flange and SRV Long insertion probe.

2.3 Other Varinline process connections

The SRV Long insertion probe is compatible with Varinline housings connection flanges type U and U-S with its corresponding Varinline F, N, or G plug depending on the pipe size. It is useful for installation in Tee or elbow pipes.

key_facts: The document provides details for achieving a longer immersion of a sensing element in a tank.
Figure 11 illustrates an exploded view of an assembly combining a Varinline tank connection flange and an SRV Long insertion probe.
The SRV Long insertion probe is designed to be compatible with Varinline housings connection flanges of types U and U-S.
The appropriate Varinline plug (F, N, or G) for the SRV Long insertion probe depends on the specific pipe size.
The SRV Long insertion probe is recommended for installation within Tee or elbow pipes.
Rheonics offers the Inline viscometer - SRV with the order code SRV-X5-A131-B Varinline N.
GEA supplies the Varinline connection flange U-S, identified by the order code TU-SN.
A drawing for 'SRV-X5-A131-B Varinline N in Tee' is accessible via a provided PDF link from rheonics.com.

Figure 13. SRV long insertion probe in the Varinline housing connection flange type U-S.
3. What is Varinline?
key_facts: The Rheonics SRV long insertion probe is designed for integration into a tee pipe.
The probe connects using a Varinline housing connector flange U-S.
A specific probe model identified is SRV-X5-A147-B Varinline N.
The Varinline housing connection flange type U-S incorporates a clamp and an O-ring for sealing.
Dimensions for the housing connection include a diameter of 131 mm (5.16 inches) and an insertion depth of 98 mm (3.84 inches).
